key_insight: BiLoG-Net is a joint segmentation-and-classification model that uses location-aware, bi-context feature learning to better detect breast masses and predict malignancy from mammograms.
clinical_relevance: By aligning lesion boundaries with malignancy assessment in one end-to-end system, it could help radiologists triage suspicious mammography cases more efficiently.
method: Encoder-decoder network with Fire-based feature extraction
Global and local context enhancement modules
Adaptive location-aware gating plus segmentation-guided attention
evidence_signal: The model was evaluated on CBIS-DDSM and INBreast, where it reported Dice scores of 94.20% and 93.10%, classification accuracies of 95.20% and 93.60%, and AUCs of 97.10% and 96.00%.
watch_item: Performance is reported on benchmark datasets, so real-world robustness, scanner variability, and prospective clinical validation still need confirmation.
paper_title: BiLoG-Net: A Bi-Context Location-Guided Network for Breast Mass Segmentation and Malignancy Classification in Mammography
